text:
These 
items must be part of the plan of care in which the home health staff is actively involved. 
For example, the patient is independent in insulin injections but the nurse visits once a 
day to change wound dressings. The wound dressings/irrigation solution may be left in 
the home between visits. Supplies such as needles, syringes, and catheters that require 
administration by a nurse should not be left in the home between visits. 

50.4.2 - Durable Medical Equipment 
(Rev. 1, 10-01-03)
